data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-11T01:43:08.743Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically regarding synchronous LINQ operations.

This is about a known breaking change in EF Core. Let me recall what I know:

In EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider, synchronous operations were never natively supported. The Cosmos DB provider is inherently async. In earlier versions, EF Core allowed "sync-over-async" — it would internally block on async calls to provide sync APIs. 

In EF Core 10 (.NET 10), there was an opt-in mechanism to allow sync-over-async (likely via a warning/configuration). But in .NET 11 / EF Core 11, the Cosmos DB provider removes sync API support entirely — calling synchronous methods like `ToList()`, `First()`, `Count()`, `SaveChanges()` throws an `InvalidOperationException`.


Actually, let me be more precise about the timeline. The Cosmos provider started warning about sync-over-async in EF Core 9, then in version 10 it allowed opting back in through configuration, but version 11 removes that opt-in entirely and throws exceptions on any synchronous LINQ calls.

The solution is straightforward: convert all the sync methods like `ToList()`, `First()`, `Count()`, and `SaveChanges()` to their async counterparts—`ToListAsync()`, `FirstAsync()`, `CountAsync()`, `SaveChangesAsync()`.
